channel.title: Feeds Student Journalism and feeds about Journalism feeds

item.title: From hobby to handmade business: Donna Wu builds Critter Trinkets
item.description: Donna Wu molds her passions into a budding art business. Wu launched her brand Critter Trinkets in October 2025 and sells clay art pieces, ranging from pins and magnets to hair clips, keychains and more.
item.source:markdown: Donna Wu molds her passions into a budding art business. Wu launched her brand Critter Trinkets in October 2025 and sells clay art pieces, ranging from pins and magnets to hair clips, keychains and more.

item.title: Are There Any AI Tools That Can Convert Photos Into Paintings?
item.description: Picture a wedding photographer who wants to offer clients a oil-painting-style portrait alongside the standard album, without commissioning an actual painter for every booking. Or a small furniture brand that...
item.source:markdown: Picture a wedding photographer who wants to offer clients a oil-painting-style portrait alongside the standard album, without commissioning an actual painter for every booking. Or a small furniture brand that...

item.title: Mexico ends a 40-year knockout drought by advancing to the Round of 16
item.description: The LA Galaxy hosted a Mexico vs. Ecuador watch party as part of its Summer of Soccer celebrations. Mexico defeated Ecuador 2-0 to advance to the Round of 16 for...
item.source:markdown: The LA Galaxy hosted a Mexico vs. Ecuador watch party as part of its Summer of Soccer celebrations. Mexico defeated Ecuador 2-0 to advance to the Round of 16 for...

item.title: Film review: ‘Supergirl’ soars with casting, visuals but loses sight of its heroine
item.description: This post was updated July 2 at 12:10 p.m. DC Studios’ new film “Supergirl” finds its kryptonite in its inability to keep the titular hero at the center of her own story.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated July 2 at 12:10 p.m._ DC Studios’ new film “Supergirl” finds its kryptonite in its inability to keep the titular hero at the center of her own story.

item.title: Are college degrees worth the cost? Study finds UC alumni earn higher wages on average
item.description: UC undergraduate degrees give students greater financial security and higher earnings compared to non-University degree-holders, according to a June study by the University’s Institutional Research and Academic Planning department.
item.source:markdown: UC undergraduate degrees give students greater financial security and higher earnings compared to non-University degree-holders, according to a June study by the University’s Institutional Research and Academic Planning department.

item.title: Gallery: Savannah Bananas somersault into Autzen Stadium, serving sold-out spectacle in Eugene
item.description: In the most widely broadcasted Banana Ball games to date – and the furthest from their Georgia origins – the Savannah Bananas and Party Animals sold out the University of Oregon’s 60,000-seat Autzen Stadium on Saturday and Sunday.
item.source:markdown: In the most widely broadcasted Banana Ball games to date – and the furthest from their Georgia origins – the Savannah Bananas and Party Animals sold out the University of Oregon’s 60,000-seat Autzen Stadium on Saturday and Sunday.

item.title: The New Immersion Race: How Realistic Worlds Are Changing Viewer Expectations on Twitch and YouTube
item.description: The visual standard that viewers expect from a Twitch or YouTube gaming stream has risen sharply over the past five years. What used to look impressive in a 2019 broadcast...
item.source:markdown: The visual standard that viewers expect from a Twitch or YouTube gaming stream has risen sharply over the past five years. What used to look impressive in a 2019 broadcast...

item.title: Now we’re getting AI fake news complaining about how AI fake news is the death of real news
item.description: Reading sad things about journalism — newspapers, especially — has been a big part of my job for almost two decades. On Monday, I came across such a story that seemed to be getting traction on social media. According to the link aggregator Sill, 18 people I follow on Bluesky had shared a link to...
item.source:markdown: Reading sad things about journalism — newspapers, especially — has been a big part of my job for almost two decades. On Monday, I came across such a story that seemed to be getting traction on social media. According to the link aggregator Sill, 18 people I follow on Bluesky had shared a link to...

item.title: Daily Bruin awarded 4 honors at 68th annual Southern California Journalism Awards
item.description: DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES – The Daily Bruin took home four awards at the 68th annual Southern California Journalism Awards ceremony Sunday evening. The Los Angeles Press Club hosted the ceremony – held at the Millennium Biltmore Hotel – which honored student media outlets and industry professionals.
item.source:markdown: DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES – The Daily Bruin took home four awards at the 68th annual Southern California Journalism Awards ceremony Sunday evening. The Los Angeles Press Club hosted the ceremony – held at the Millennium Biltmore Hotel – which honored student media outlets and industry professionals.

item.title: One Standard for Everyone. How Vasyl Zahorodniuk Judged the Startups at South Summit 2026
item.description:
One Standard for Everyone. How Vasyl Zahorodniuk Judged the Startups at South Summit 2026
You can spot a good startup-competition judge by one thing. They measure everyone by the same yardstick.
item.source:markdown:
One Standard for Everyone. How Vasyl Zahorodniuk Judged the Startups at South Summit 2026
You can spot a good startup-competition judge by one thing. They measure everyone by the same yardstick.

item.title: New faces, fresh hopes: UCLA women’s soccer embraces new era with 5 transfers
item.description: This year, UCLA women’s soccer is all about welcoming new changes. Boasting a new head coach, Gof Boyoko, and two incoming freshmen, and, most notably, five strong transfers, the team has its eyes set on a new road to the NCAA championships.
item.source:markdown: This year, UCLA women’s soccer is all about welcoming new changes. Boasting a new head coach, Gof Boyoko, and two incoming freshmen, and, most notably, five strong transfers, the team has its eyes set on a new road to the NCAA championships.

item.title: Gov. Gavin Newsom signs state budget, granting UC $350 million funding increase
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom signed California’s state budget for the upcoming fiscal year Monday, increasing the UC’s funding to $350 million. The budget also preserves a nearly $130 million deferral to the University until the 2027-28 fiscal year.
item.source:markdown: Gov. Gavin Newsom signed California’s state budget for the upcoming fiscal year Monday, increasing the UC’s funding to $350 million. The budget also preserves a nearly $130 million deferral to the University until the 2027-28 fiscal year.

item.title: Gallery: A celebration of tradition: Root[d brings Indian wedding culture to UCLA
item.description: UCLA Root[d, a South Asian urban fusion dance team, hosted Mock Mehndi on May 20 at the James West Alumni Center. Performers recreated a traditional Indian pre-wedding ceremony at the event.
item.source:markdown: UCLA Root\[d, a South Asian urban fusion dance team, hosted Mock Mehndi on May 20 at the James West Alumni Center. Performers recreated a traditional Indian pre-wedding ceremony at the event.

item.title: Gallery: Daniela Andrade brings intimate storytelling to El Rey Theatre on her “Oda Tour”
item.description: Daniela Andrade is an ode to independent artistry on the “Oda Tour.” The Honduran-Canadian singer-songwriter returned to Los Angeles after nearly a decade for a performance at the El Rey Theatre on Friday.
item.source:markdown: Daniela Andrade is an ode to independent artistry on the “Oda Tour.” The Honduran-Canadian singer-songwriter returned to Los Angeles after nearly a decade for a performance at the El Rey Theatre on Friday.

item.title: Fact-checkers reckon with shrinking budgets and growing AI threats at GlobalFact 2026
item.description: “Welcome to Vilnius, the city where we face cognitive warfare for real,” Robertas Kaunas, Lithuania’s minister of national defense, said in his opening remarks at this year’s GlobalFact. On June 17, hundreds of fact-checkers assembled in Vilnius for the annual conference of the International Fact-Checking Network (IFCN), part of the Poynter Institute. Roughly 180 fact-checking...
item.source:markdown: “Welcome to Vilnius, the city where we face cognitive warfare for real,” Robertas Kaunas, Lithuania’s minister of national defense, said in his opening remarks at this year’s GlobalFact. On June 17, hundreds of fact-checkers assembled in Vilnius for the annual conference of the International Fact-Checking Network (IFCN), part of the Poynter Institute. Roughly 180 fact-checking...

item.title: Opinion: UCLA’s finance alumni network values genuine relationships, not transactions
item.description: At a finance career fair, desperation is easy to spot. Finance has long been an industry in which connections often determine your professional opportunities. UCLA students are usually at an advantage here: They have a vast, engaged network of alumni who want to help fellow Bruins because that identity still means something to them.
item.source:markdown: At a finance career fair, desperation is easy to spot. Finance has long been an industry in which connections often determine your professional opportunities. UCLA students are usually at an advantage here: They have a vast, engaged network of alumni who want to help fellow Bruins because that identity still means something to them.

item.title: What short news videos reveal about the Messi–Ronaldo debate — and news distribution today
item.description: The World Cup is the gift that keeps on giving (to me). I was swiping through Instagram Stories during halftime of the Canada vs. Qatar World Cup match when I saw a post about a study about the political indicators of a person’s preference of soccer stars Lionel Messi, the Argentine forward, or Cristiano Ronaldo,...
item.source:markdown: The World Cup is the gift that keeps on giving (to me). I was swiping through Instagram Stories during halftime of the Canada vs. Qatar World Cup match when I saw a post about a study about the political indicators of a person’s preference of soccer stars Lionel Messi, the Argentine forward, or Cristiano Ronaldo,...

item.title: OPINION: The FIFA World Cup group stage matchups have come to a close
item.description: Over the past few weeks, more than half of the FIFA World Cup matches have been played. With the first round of 32 matchup complete, here are the top five...
item.source:markdown: Over the past few weeks, more than half of the FIFA World Cup matches have been played. With the first round of 32 matchup complete, here are the top five...

item.title: Op-ed: UCLA should prioritize reducing car dependency over expanding parking
item.description: Contrary to the beliefs of the students and faculty interviewed in Avital Abramov’s June 7 piece, “Students, faculty frustrated with high parking costs, limited permit access,” UCLA should not increase parking availability or decrease parking fees.
item.source:markdown: Contrary to the beliefs of the students and faculty interviewed in Avital Abramov’s June 7 piece, “Students, faculty frustrated with high parking costs, limited permit access,” UCLA should not increase parking availability or decrease parking fees.

item.title: Capital Weather is dropping the “Gang” and going independent
item.description: If you have ever lived in the Washington, D.C. metro area, or had the misfortune of being stuck there during a snowstorm, there’s a good chance you’ve heard of the Capital Weather Gang. For eighteen years they were The Washington Post’s oracles of the atmosphere, covering local weather on the Post website and on social...
item.source:markdown: If you have ever lived in the Washington, D.C. metro area, or had the misfortune of being stuck there during a snowstorm, there’s a good chance you’ve heard of the Capital Weather Gang. For eighteen years they were The Washington Post’s oracles of the atmosphere, covering local weather on the Post website and on social...

item.title: With the Expansion of Legalized Online Betting, College Students Face New Risks and Knowledge Gaps
item.description: Online betting is no longer something students encounter occasionally; it has become part of the digital environment many move through every day. As more U.S. states legalize sports betting and online casino platforms, gambling has become easier to access, harder to avoid and more deeply embedded in everyday media.
item.source:markdown: Online betting is no longer something students encounter occasionally; it has become part of the digital environment many move through every day. As more U.S. states legalize sports betting and online casino platforms, gambling has become easier to access, harder to avoid and more deeply embedded in everyday media.

item.title: Why Daily Login Bonuses Are Becoming More Popular With Student Gamers
item.description:
Why Daily Login Bonuses Are Becoming More Popular With Student Gamers
For many student gamers, tapping into a game to claim a daily bonus has become a regular part of campus routines.
item.source:markdown:
Why Daily Login Bonuses Are Becoming More Popular With Student Gamers
For many student gamers, tapping into a game to claim a daily bonus has become a regular part of campus routines.

item.title: Don’t be spooked by this supernatural UCLA course
item.description: Aliens, Psychics and Ghosts – a UCLA course taught by professor David Delgado Shorter – has everything from teacher-led meditations to lively debates about paranormal legends.
item.source:markdown: Aliens, Psychics and Ghosts – a UCLA course taught by professor David Delgado Shorter – has everything from teacher-led meditations to lively debates about paranormal legends.

item.title: Sinclairity: World Cup excitement can kick-start renewed support for UCLA men’s soccer
item.description: This post was updated June 28 at 5:26 p.m. 70,492. That’s how many people attended the United States men’s national team’s 2026 World Cup opener at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles on June 12.
item.source:markdown: This post was updated June 28 at 5:26 p.m. 70,492. That’s how many people attended the United States men’s national team’s 2026 World Cup opener at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles on June 12.

item.title: Second Take: ‘Obsession,’ ‘Backrooms’ prove fresh stories spark conversations, fill theaters
item.description: This post was updated June 30 at 11:50 a.m. Warning: Spoilers ahead Nights at the movie theater are back in style. Generation Z directors Kane Parsons and Curry Barker seem to have cracked the code to refilling red velvet recliners with their blockbuster horrors, “Backrooms” and “Obsession.” Both indie films have effectively dwarfed their micro-budgets at the box office, with each grossing over 300 million dollars since their respective releases in May.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated June 30 at 11:50 a.m._ Warning: Spoilers ahead Nights at the movie theater are back in style. Generation Z directors Kane Parsons and Curry Barker seem to have cracked the code to refilling red velvet recliners with their blockbuster horrors, “Backrooms” and “Obsession.” Both indie films have effectively dwarfed their micro-budgets at the box office, with each grossing over 300 million dollars since their respective releases in May.

item.title: Soccer fans believe hydration breaks are ruining the momentum of World Cup matches
item.description: The FIFA World Cup enters the knockout stage today with the Round of 32 kicking off, as South Africa faces Canada. Many soccer fans are excited for the upcoming games,...
item.source:markdown: The FIFA World Cup enters the knockout stage today with the Round of 32 kicking off, as South Africa faces Canada. Many soccer fans are excited for the upcoming games,...

item.title: UCLA women’s tennis bolsters doubles play by adding transfer Jessica Bernales
item.description: Between the doubles alleys, athletes pair up to work toward success and coaches experiment with different lineups, probing for the perfect combination. And those possibilities increased dramatically for UCLA women’s tennis this summer, thanks to the signing of rising junior Jessica Bernales.
item.source:markdown: Between the doubles alleys, athletes pair up to work toward success and coaches experiment with different lineups, probing for the perfect combination. And those possibilities increased dramatically for UCLA women’s tennis this summer, thanks to the signing of rising junior Jessica Bernales.

item.title: Conservative group files lawsuit against UC for alleged free speech violations
item.description: This post was updated June 29 at 2:25 p.m. A conservative group filed a lawsuit against the UC on June 18, alleging that the University’s sexual harassment policies and anti-discrimination training violated free speech.
item.source:markdown: This post was updated June 29 at 2:25 p.m. A conservative group filed a lawsuit against the UC on June 18, alleging that the University’s sexual harassment policies and anti-discrimination training violated free speech.

item.title: Former UCLA men’s basketball center William Kyle III lands contract with Lakers
item.description: A third former Bruin joins the professional ranks. Former UCLA and Syracuse men’s basketball forward/center William Kyle III signed an Exhibit 10 contract with the Los Angeles Lakers on Thursday after going undrafted in the 2026 NBA Draft, ESPN reported.
item.source:markdown: A third former Bruin joins the professional ranks. Former UCLA and Syracuse men’s basketball forward/center William Kyle III signed an Exhibit 10 contract with the Los Angeles Lakers on Thursday after going undrafted in the 2026 NBA Draft, ESPN reported.

item.title: Op-ed: Protecting older adults from financial scams starts with the younger generations
item.description: Last spring, my grandma sent me an innocuous text: “Dang weather! It’s too stormy to drive, but I need to pick up some Target gift cards to pay my DirecTV bill.” Unbeknownst to her, the DIRECTV representative she had spoken to was a scammer who convinced her she could pay her bill with gift cards.
item.source:markdown: Last spring, my grandma sent me an innocuous text: “Dang weather! It’s too stormy to drive, but I need to pick up some Target gift cards to pay my DirecTV bill.” Unbeknownst to her, the DIRECTV representative she had spoken to was a scammer who convinced her she could pay her bill with gift cards.

item.title: UCLA club Project Medusa hosts creative workshops for domestic violence survivors
item.description: A new club is using art to support Asian American survivors of domestic violence. Project Medusa, a student-led initiative founded in February, puts on creative workshops for domestic violence survivors in Los Angeles.
item.source:markdown: A new club is using art to support Asian American survivors of domestic violence. Project Medusa, a student-led initiative founded in February, puts on creative workshops for domestic violence survivors in Los Angeles.

item.title: iGaming Regulation & UX: John Gold Insights — BetPokies NZ
item.description:
From UX to Regulation — John Gold’s Takeaways from the iGaming Strategy Conference
The iGaming Strategy Conference rarely produces consensus. Operators, regulators, platform engineers, and compliance officers occupy the same rooms for three days, and they rarely leave without agreeing on much.
item.source:markdown:
From UX to Regulation — John Gold’s Takeaways from the iGaming Strategy Conference
The iGaming Strategy Conference rarely produces consensus. Operators, regulators, platform engineers, and compliance officers occupy the same rooms for three days, and they rarely leave without agreeing on much.

item.title: How UCLA students are navigating the shift to cashless campus transactions
item.source:markdown:
How UCLA students are navigating the shift to cashless campus transactions
Paying for lunch at Bruin Café or printing a paper in the library used to mean having a few dollars on hand.

item.title: Women’s basketball assistant coach Tasha Brown leaves UCLA for Washington
item.description: One week after head coach Cori Close strengthened her staff, a key member announced her departure from the program. Former UCLA women’s basketball assistant coach Tasha Brown left Westwood and joined Washington’s coaching staff, as announced June 26.
item.source:markdown: One week after head coach Cori Close strengthened her staff, a key member announced her departure from the program. Former UCLA women’s basketball assistant coach Tasha Brown left Westwood and joined Washington’s coaching staff, as announced June 26.

item.title: Bond with $350 million for student housing to appear on general election ballot
item.description: This post was updated June 26 at 3:42 p.m. Californians will vote on a bond that would allocate $350,000,000 to new affordable student housing projects in the November election.  Senate Bill 417 – the Veterans and Affordable Housing Bond Act of 2026 – passed the California State Senate and was approved by Gov.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated June 26 at 3:42 p.m._ Californians will vote on a bond that would allocate $350,000,000 to new affordable student housing projects in the November election. Senate Bill 417 – the Veterans and Affordable Housing Bond Act of 2026 – passed the California State Senate and was approved by Gov.

item.title: Gallery: ‘Love yourself, no matter who you are’: 2026 WeHo Pride Parade celebrates in style
item.description: The streets of West Hollywood were bursting with bubbles and fluttering confetti. The WeHo Pride Parade took place along Santa Monica Boulevard on June 7. UCLA and UCLA Health were represented along the parade route and among the attendees.
item.source:markdown: The streets of West Hollywood were bursting with bubbles and fluttering confetti. The WeHo Pride Parade took place along Santa Monica Boulevard on June 7. UCLA and UCLA Health were represented along the parade route and among the attendees.

item.title: Q&A: LA Pride centers humanity, hope through celebration, action
item.description: This post was updated June 25 at 11:31 p.m. There is Pride even amid prejudice. Christopher Street West held its 56th annual Los Angeles Pride Parade in Hollywood on June 14.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated June 25 at 11:31 p.m._ There is Pride even amid prejudice. Christopher Street West held its 56th annual Los Angeles Pride Parade in Hollywood on June 14.

item.title: Concert review: Madison Beer’s “locket tour” enchants with alluring vocals, ethereal visuals
item.description: Madison Beer took fans through the twists and turns of her “locket tour,” winning hearts with her captivating voice. The American singer-songwriter brought her largest headlining tour to date to the Kia Forum on Wednesday night.
item.source:markdown: Madison Beer took fans through the twists and turns of her “locket tour,” winning hearts with her captivating voice. The American singer-songwriter brought her largest headlining tour to date to the Kia Forum on Wednesday night.

item.title: UCLA comparative literature department to offer health humanities minor in fall
item.description: UCLA will offer a minor in health humanities starting this fall. The minor, housed under the comparative literature department, is an interdisciplinary program that examines how health and medicine are shaped by literature, culture, history and social experience, according to the department’s website.
item.source:markdown: UCLA will offer a minor in health humanities starting this fall. The minor, housed under the comparative literature department, is an interdisciplinary program that examines how health and medicine are shaped by literature, culture, history and social experience, according to the department’s website.

item.title: Higher minimum wage offers some relief amid California’s rising cost of living
item.description: Anneliese Fiegl, a senior student supervisor at Kerckhoff Coffeehouse, uses most of her earnings to pay for tuition and educational expenses. The rising third-year biochemistry student earns $20.75 per hour overseeing other employees for around 10 hours each week.
item.source:markdown: Anneliese Fiegl, a senior student supervisor at Kerckhoff Coffeehouse, uses most of her earnings to pay for tuition and educational expenses. The rising third-year biochemistry student earns $20.75 per hour overseeing other employees for around 10 hours each week.

item.title: Beehiiv’s new Cloudflare partnership gives indie journalists a new level of control over AI crawlers
item.description: Over the past year, major news publishers have taken aggressive anti-scraping measures to curb the rise of AI crawlers. So far, most of these backend interventions have been out of reach for independent journalists who publish on third-party newsletter platforms. That is changing with a new partnership announced on Tuesday between Beehiiv and Cloudflare, the...
item.source:markdown: Over the past year, major news publishers have taken aggressive anti-scraping measures to curb the rise of AI crawlers. So far, most of these backend interventions have been out of reach for independent journalists who publish on third-party newsletter platforms. That is changing with a new partnership announced on Tuesday between Beehiiv and Cloudflare, the...

item.title: Mexico Claims Group A Lead With 3-0 Victory Over Czechia as Fans Celebrate at Viva Futbol
item.description: The city of San Fernando hosted its second Viva Futbol watch party as Mexico faced Czechia. The Group A match thrilled Mexican fans as Mexico cruised to a 3-0 victory,...
item.source:markdown: The city of San Fernando hosted its second Viva Futbol watch party as Mexico faced Czechia. The Group A match thrilled Mexican fans as Mexico cruised to a 3-0 victory,...

item.title: Sabor Colombiano hosts Colombian soccer fans during World Cup
item.description: Colombian soccer fans gather at Sabor Colombiano to watch Colombia’s World Cup match against DR Congo. Colombia won 1-0 and advanced to the next round.
item.source:markdown: Colombian soccer fans gather at Sabor Colombiano to watch Colombia’s World Cup match against DR Congo. Colombia won 1-0 and advanced to the next round.

item.title: The demand for news video is growing (and that’s a good thing for publishers)
item.description: News video is as popular as ever. Publishers who remember the “pivot to video” from a decade ago might wince at the thought, remembering how misleading metrics led many publishers to invest in video content. But evidence from our newly published Digital News Report suggests the demand for news video is growing. A decade on,...
item.source:markdown: News video is as popular as ever. Publishers who remember the “pivot to video” from a decade ago might wince at the thought, remembering how misleading metrics led many publishers to invest in video content. But evidence from our newly published Digital News Report suggests the demand for news video is growing. A decade on,...
item.category: Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism

item.title: Full Fact is battling AI-generated elections content with AI tools of its own
item.description: As campaign videos go, it was a classic. To the sound of a soulful pop ballad, a smiling politician meets voters in the street, chats to school children and visits a hospital. The only problem? The scenes shown in the video weren’t real. They were AI-generated, and shared and labeled as “illustrative” by an independent...
item.source:markdown: As campaign videos go, it was a classic. To the sound of a soulful pop ballad, a smiling politician meets voters in the street, chats to school children and visits a hospital. The only problem? The scenes shown in the video weren’t real. They were AI-generated, and shared and labeled as “illustrative” by an independent...

item.title: Brooklyn Nets select UCLA’s Tyler Bilodeau with No. 43 pick
item.description: The NBA just got one Bruin larger. UCLA men’s basketball’s Tyler Bilodeau was drafted 43rd overall by the Brooklyn Nets in the second round of the 2026 NBA Draft on Wednesday.
item.source:markdown: The NBA just got one Bruin larger. UCLA men’s basketball’s Tyler Bilodeau was drafted 43rd overall by the Brooklyn Nets in the second round of the 2026 NBA Draft on Wednesday.

item.title: Gallery: Grupo Estudiantil Oaxaqueño celebrates Oaxacan culture with annual Guelaguetza
item.description: Vibrant handmade dresses spun in unison to the beat of drums and trumpets in Bruin Plaza. Grupo Estudiantil Oaxaqueño de UCLA hosted its annual Guelaguetza performance May 30, featuring dancers from El Ballet Folklórico Oaxaqueño Nueva Antequera, Maqueos Music Academy and Diablos Nueva Unión.
item.source:markdown: Vibrant handmade dresses spun in unison to the beat of drums and trumpets in Bruin Plaza. Grupo Estudiantil Oaxaqueño de UCLA hosted its annual Guelaguetza performance May 30, featuring dancers from El Ballet Folklórico Oaxaqueño Nueva Antequera, Maqueos Music Academy and Diablos Nueva Unión.

item.title: Overwhelmed by news on social? SaySo is betting a smaller, vetted creator feed is the answer
item.description: Social media is overwhelming. The amount of information, misinformation, and slop makes it hard for the average news consumer to wade through the deluge of posts and know who to trust. According to the Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism, more and more people are turning to creators for news. About 27% of adults...
item.source:markdown: Social media is overwhelming. The amount of information, misinformation, and slop makes it hard for the average news consumer to wade through the deluge of posts and know who to trust. According to the Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism, more and more people are turning to creators for news. About 27% of adults...

item.title: Rotary Club luncheon highlights Iranian history, ongoing conflict
item.description: An activist and Westwood restaurant owner raised awareness about historical and current conflicts in Iran at a Thursday luncheon. The most recent United States-Iran conflict began Feb.
item.source:markdown: An activist and Westwood restaurant owner raised awareness about historical and current conflicts in Iran at a Thursday luncheon. The most recent United States-Iran conflict began Feb.

item.title: USC researchers find communication gap between AI and humans
item.description:
The team concluded artificial intelligence interprets words like “maybe” or “probably” differently than us.

The team concluded artificial intelligence interprets words like “maybe” or “probably” differently than us.

item.title: Internet fame, musical acclaim: 4 LGBTQIA+ icons who attended USC
item.description:
Learn about some famous LGBTQIA+ alums as Pride Month comes to a close.

The post Internet fame, musical acclaim: 4 LGBTQIA+ icons who attended USC appeared first on Daily Trojan.
item.source:markdown:
Learn about some famous LGBTQIA+ alums as Pride Month comes to a close.
The post [Internet fame, musical acclaim: 4 LGBTQIA+ icons who attended USC](https://dailytrojan.com/2026/06/24/lgbtqia-icons-who-attended-usc/) appeared first on [Daily Trojan](https://dailytrojan.com).

item.title: Former Bruin Aday Mara drafted 12th to Thunder after NCAA title run
item.description: This post was updated June 23 at 7:27 p.m. Just a day after former Michigan head coach Dusty May left college basketball for the NBA, three of his players – one a former Bruin – will make the professional jump with him.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated June 23 at 7:27 p.m._ Just a day after former Michigan head coach Dusty May left college basketball for the NBA, three of his players – one a former Bruin – will make the professional jump with him.

item.title: The British government wants to force more trustworthy news into your doomscrolling
item.description: The British government is asking social media companies to put more news — real news, produced by public service broadcasters like the BBC — high up in people’s feeds. And if companies refuse, it’ll pass laws to require it. That’s the main takeaway from a new report issued Tuesday on a host of issues relating...
item.source:markdown: The British government is asking social media companies to put more news — real news, produced by public service broadcasters like the BBC — high up in people’s feeds. And if companies refuse, it’ll pass laws to require it. That’s the main takeaway from a new report issued Tuesday on a host of issues relating...

item.title: The use of AI chatbots for news is on the rise — but not everywhere
item.description: This year’s Digital News Report marked a notable milestone: for the first time, social and video networks overtook news publishers as a source of news globally. As audiences continue to migrate toward digital intermediaries for news, AI chatbots are emerging as the next platform to watch. Findings from our 2026 survey show that one in...
item.source:markdown: This year’s Digital News Report marked a notable milestone: for the first time, social and video networks overtook news publishers as a source of news globally. As audiences continue to migrate toward digital intermediaries for news, AI chatbots are emerging as the next platform to watch. Findings from our 2026 survey show that one in...
item.category: Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism

item.title: Editorial Cartoon: “if there only were a second one”
Dizon’s Disposition: John Savage’s nuanced stance on college baseball
item.description: A high school senior in Reno, Nevada, threw 24 consecutive no-hit innings in 1983. The right-hander finished the year with a 1.16 ERA, and the New York Yankees came calling in June.
item.source:markdown: A high school senior in Reno, Nevada, threw 24 consecutive no-hit innings in 1983. The right-hander finished the year with a 1.16 ERA, and the New York Yankees came calling in June.

item.title: Alilah Alvarado transfers from BYU to UCLA gymnastics for final season
item.description: This post was updated June 23 at 1:39 p.m. UCLA gymnastics landed its first transfer of the offseason Monday.  Rising senior Alilah Alvarado announced her move from Provo, Utah, to Westwood on Instagram.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated June 23 at 1:39 p.m._ UCLA gymnastics landed its first transfer of the offseason Monday. Rising senior Alilah Alvarado announced her move from Provo, Utah, to Westwood on Instagram.

item.title: Design and Media Arts seniors turn personal stories into immersive art at Fruition
item.description: UCLA’s Design and Media Arts program brought its seniors’ ideas to fruition in an interactive exhibit. On June 4, the undergraduate Design and Media Arts program (DMA) hosted a multimedia exhibit featuring projects from the Capstone Senior Project class, which DMA students are required to take in their final quarter.
item.source:markdown: UCLA’s Design and Media Arts program brought its seniors’ ideas to fruition in an interactive exhibit. On June 4, the undergraduate Design and Media Arts program (DMA) hosted a multimedia exhibit featuring projects from the Capstone Senior Project class, which DMA students are required to take in their final quarter.

item.title: The Boston Globe published a full-page ad thanking the Tartan Army for “the laughter, the bagpipes, and the memories”
item.description: You’ve seen the headlines, or TikToks — the traffic cones on statues, the pubs drunk dry, the throngs of men in kilts. The Tartan Army took Boston by storm in the best possible way last week during Scotland’s run of games in Foxborough, and The Boston Globe has chronicled the happy chaos with zeal. “As...
item.source:markdown: You’ve seen the headlines, or TikToks — the traffic cones on statues, the pubs drunk dry, the throngs of men in kilts. The Tartan Army took Boston by storm in the best possible way last week during Scotland’s run of games in Foxborough, and The Boston Globe has chronicled the happy chaos with zeal. “As...

item.title: Book reviews are an endangered species. So this independent bookstore decided to start publishing its own.
item.description: When The Washington Post’s management axed Book World in February, it wasn’t just one casualty among hundreds of layoffs; it was the latest high-profile death blow to a newspaper book review section in more than two decades of a thousand cuts. The AP stopped publishing book reviews last fall. Metro dailies like the San Francisco...
item.source:markdown: When The Washington Post’s management axed Book World in February, it wasn’t just one casualty among hundreds of layoffs; it was the latest high-profile death blow to a newspaper book review section in more than two decades of a thousand cuts. The AP stopped publishing book reviews last fall. Metro dailies like the San Francisco...

item.title: How Digital Rewards Keep Casual Games Engaging
item.description: Daily rewards are a familiar part of casual gaming. Players collect a bonus, maintain a streak, or receive virtual currency simply for returning to a game.
item.source:markdown: Daily rewards are a familiar part of casual gaming. Players collect a bonus, maintain a streak, or receive virtual currency simply for returning to a game.

item.title: Bet365 Preparing to Join the Alberta Gambling Market at Launch
item.description: Global gambling operator bet365 has applied to be among companies launching in Alberta when it opens its regulated online casino and sports betting market on July 13.
item.source:markdown: Global gambling operator bet365 has applied to be among companies launching in Alberta when it opens its regulated online casino and sports betting market on July 13.

item.title: Breaking down Tyler Bilodeau’s prospect profile ahead of the 2026 NBA Draft
item.description: The 2026 NBA Draft is set to take place June 23-24, during which 60 new names will be called into the league. UCLA men’s basketball’s most recent draft selection was former center Adem Bona, who was selected by the Philadelphia 76ers with the 41st pick in 2024.
item.source:markdown: The 2026 NBA Draft is set to take place June 23-24, during which 60 new names will be called into the league. UCLA men’s basketball’s most recent draft selection was former center Adem Bona, who was selected by the Philadelphia 76ers with the 41st pick in 2024.

item.title: Editorial: UCLA’s communication failures erode trust, underscore need for transparency
item.description: A journalist sends an email to a UCLA administrator. The administrator then forwards it to the university’s Strategic Communications team. Days later, the journalist gets back a carefully sculpted and minimally detailed statement.
item.source:markdown: A journalist sends an email to a UCLA administrator. The administrator then forwards it to the university’s Strategic Communications team. Days later, the journalist gets back a carefully sculpted and minimally detailed statement.

item.title: 3-year starting pitcher Landon Stump leaves UCLA baseball for transfer portal
item.description: This post was updated June 20 at 5:04 p.m. UCLA baseball’s Sunday starter for the majority of the past three seasons – junior right-hander Landon Stump – has entered the transfer portal, On3 reported Saturday morning.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated June 20 at 5:04 p.m._ UCLA baseball’s Sunday starter for the majority of the past three seasons – junior right-hander Landon Stump – has entered the transfer portal, On3 reported Saturday morning.

item.title: Soccer Fans Gather at a British Pub to Watch the United States vs. Australia Match
item.description: The Robin Hood British Pub continues its tradition of showing FIFA World Cup matches as the United States tries to clinch a knockout-round spot for the second consecutive World Cup.
item.source:markdown: The Robin Hood British Pub continues its tradition of showing FIFA World Cup matches as the United States tries to clinch a knockout-round spot for the second consecutive World Cup.

item.title: Mexico fans gather in Boyle Heights for Mexico-South Korea match

item.source:markdown: Angel City FC and Metro hosted a Mexico-South Korea watch party at Mariachi Plaza in Boyle Heights on June 18. Mexican soccer player Luis Romo scored the game-winning goal in...

item.title: UCLA alumnus’s pioneering endometriosis treatment plans to enter clinical trials
item.description: This post was updated June 19 at 12:08 a.m. A new endometriosis treatment developed by a UCLA alumnus will enter clinical trials after receiving Food and Drug Administration clearance in March.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated June 19 at 12:08 a.m._ A new endometriosis treatment developed by a UCLA alumnus will enter clinical trials after receiving Food and Drug Administration clearance in March.

item.title: UCLA track and field concludes season at NCAA outdoor championships
item.description: UCLA track and field sent eleven student-athletes to compete in the 2026 NCAA outdoor championships in Eugene from June 10 to 13. The team left Hayward Field, but it did not leave empty handed.
item.source:markdown: UCLA track and field sent eleven student-athletes to compete in the 2026 NCAA outdoor championships in Eugene from June 10 to 13. The team left Hayward Field, but it did not leave empty handed.

item.title: UCLA men’s soccer commit named Gatorade Player of the Year
item.description: A team’s success begins in the backline. And the Bruins’ new addition could be a key piece of this. Incoming UCLA men’s soccer commit defender Marcus Jackson was named the Gatorade National Boys Soccer Player of the Year on Wednesday, the highest individual honor of the sport at the high school level.
item.source:markdown: A team’s success begins in the backline. And the Bruins’ new addition could be a key piece of this. Incoming UCLA men’s soccer commit defender Marcus Jackson was named the Gatorade National Boys Soccer Player of the Year on Wednesday, the highest individual honor of the sport at the high school level.

item.title: Shannon LeBeauf rejoins UCLA women’s basketball as assistant head coach
item.description: Just one month after Cori Close inked a new deal to remain with the Bruins following a national championship, the head honcho has set out to reinforce her already prestigious staff.
item.source:markdown: Just one month after Cori Close inked a new deal to remain with the Bruins following a national championship, the head honcho has set out to reinforce her already prestigious staff.

item.title: States with less local news have higher rates of loneliness
item.description: The 2026 Local Journalist Index, released this week by Muck Rack and the Rebuild Local News Coalition, contains a fair bit of bad news that may not surprise you: The number of working local journalists has plummeted over the last 25 years (and continued to decline last year), and the little coverage that’s left tends...
item.source:markdown: The 2026 Local Journalist Index, released this week by Muck Rack and the Rebuild Local News Coalition, contains a fair bit of bad news that may not surprise you: The number of working local journalists has plummeted over the last 25 years (and continued to decline last year), and the little coverage that’s left tends...

item.title: How should news organizations label their AI use for audiences? New studies suggest some answers
item.description: As we’ve reported previously, AI-generated journalism provokes distrust from readers, with audiences favoring a human touch. That might seem obvious, but a lot of questions lie buried in that broad statement. First, with so many ways to use AI, what does “AI-generated” mean, and how do audiences respond to different types and levels of AI...
item.source:markdown: As we’ve reported previously, AI-generated journalism provokes distrust from readers, with audiences favoring a human touch. That might seem obvious, but a lot of questions lie buried in that broad statement. First, with so many ways to use AI, what does “AI-generated” mean, and how do audiences respond to different types and levels of AI...

item.title: State legislature passes budget with funding increase for UC
item.description: The California State Legislature approved a state budget Monday to increase the UC’s funding by approximately $350 million for the upcoming fiscal year. Legislators will negotiate with Gov.
item.source:markdown: The California State Legislature approved a state budget Monday to increase the UC’s funding by approximately $350 million for the upcoming fiscal year. Legislators will negotiate with Gov.

item.title: Building your first audience: how to choose the right email tool from the start
item.description: Embarking on an independent journey as a student entrepreneur or creative means juggling multiple responsibilities. With ambitions soaring and limited time, establishing a central channel for outreach becomes essential. Even...
item.source:markdown: Embarking on an independent journey as a student entrepreneur or creative means juggling multiple responsibilities. With ambitions soaring and limited time, establishing a central channel for outreach becomes essential. Even...

item.title: Substack is launching a sponsorship program
item.description: On Monday, Substack announced a framework for a native sponsorship program, along with “Creator Kits” to help Substack users build media kits for potential brand partners. (It’s also hired its first head of brand sponsorships, Axios reported.) These tools will make it easier for writers to increase their revenue while continuing to build their subscription...
item.source:markdown: On Monday, Substack announced a framework for a native sponsorship program, along with “Creator Kits” to help Substack users build media kits for potential brand partners. (It’s also hired its first head of brand sponsorships, Axios reported.) These tools will make it easier for writers to increase their revenue while continuing to build their subscription...

item.title: Gallery: Shakira resumes her US tour at the Intuit Dome with classic hits, new songs
item.source:markdown:
Concertgoers won’t have to cry this summer.
Shakira resumed the U.S. leg of her “Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour” on Saturday. The limited run of the Colombian singer-songwriter’s discography-spanning tour is being staged in smaller venues in comparison to the tour’s original record-breaking stadium shows.

item.title: How Tripo AI Is Building Interactive Worlds
item.description: This post was created by Bruin Media Group, the marketing section of the Daily Bruin. The editorial staff of the Daily Bruin played no role in the creation of this post and its content.
item.source:markdown: _This post was created by Bruin Media Group, the marketing section of the Daily Bruin. The editorial staff of the Daily Bruin played no role in the creation of this post and its content._

item.title: News sites are the new newspapers: People are abandoning them for social media
item.description: News sites are rapidly becoming the newspapers of the digital age. And you know what happened to newspapers. Worldwide, people in all age groups are ditching news sites and publishers’ apps in favor of social media and video networks. In fact, 18- to 34-year-olds are abandoning content on publishers’ platforms even more quickly than they’re...
item.source:markdown: News sites are rapidly becoming the newspapers of the digital age. And you know what happened to newspapers. Worldwide, people in all age groups are ditching news sites and publishers’ apps in favor of social media and video networks. In fact, 18- to 34-year-olds are abandoning content on publishers’ platforms even more quickly than they’re...
item.category: Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism

item.title: The Newsground turns to coffee to fund investigative journalism
item.description: It’s a beloved newspaper cliché: The reader sitting at the kitchen table sipping a hot cup of coffee while leisurely scanning the newspaper. The news consumption experience, for most people, hasn’t looked like that for a long time. But a new publication wants to update the news-and-coffee experience for the modern era. Enter: The Newsground,...
item.source:markdown: It’s a beloved newspaper cliché: The reader sitting at the kitchen table sipping a hot cup of coffee while leisurely scanning the newspaper. The news consumption experience, for most people, hasn’t looked like that for a long time. But a new publication wants to update the news-and-coffee experience for the modern era. Enter: The Newsground,...

item.title: Dancer University teaches local salsa, bachata classes students don’t want to drop
item.description: After teaching Latin dances for 16 years, UCLA alumnus Nicole Gil is looking to build community in Westwood through new classes. Dancer University, a Los Angeles-based Latin dance organization, hosted a salsa and bachata class in Westwood on May 23.
item.source:markdown: After teaching Latin dances for 16 years, UCLA alumnus Nicole Gil is looking to build community in Westwood through new classes. Dancer University, a Los Angeles-based Latin dance organization, hosted a salsa and bachata class in Westwood on May 23.

item.title: LA voters approve 3 tax measures in June primary election
item.description: This post was updated June 14 at 11:58 a.m. 3 Los Angeles ballot measures that will raise taxes passed in the June 2 primary election, according to NBC.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated June 14 at 11:58 a.m._ 3 Los Angeles ballot measures that will raise taxes passed in the June 2 primary election, according to NBC.

item.title: Erickson, Goldsmith advance to November general for California Senate District 24
item.description: Democrats John Erickson and Brian Goldsmith will proceed to the November general election for California State Senate District 24, which encompasses UCLA’s campus and surrounding communities.
item.source:markdown: Democrats John Erickson and Brian Goldsmith will proceed to the November general election for California State Senate District 24, which encompasses UCLA’s campus and surrounding communities.

item.title: ‘Lead with intellect, humility and heart’: Lessons from 2026 commencement speakers

item.source:markdown: _This post was updated June 22 at 12:51 p.m._ Speakers called on graduating students to stay resilient amid political division and academic challenges during the annual College of Letters and Science commencement ceremony Friday.

item.title: Soccer fans celebrate the start of the World Cup at BMO Stadium
item.description: Angelenos and tourists came together to kickoff the World Cup with a day of watch parties, tournament, and live music hosted by BMO and Adidas. The event concluded with live...
item.source:markdown: Angelenos and tourists came together to kickoff the World Cup with a day of watch parties, tournament, and live music hosted by BMO and Adidas. The event concluded with live...

item.title: Keke Palmer announces 5-year residency with TFT, plans to spotlight student work
item.description: This post was updated June 12 at 5:11 p.m. Keke Palmer is taking on a new role in Westwood. The Emmy Award winner announced Thursday morning that she will begin a five-year Artist in Residency program with UCLA’s School of Theater, Film and Television, titled “From Blocking to Broadcast.” The program will go into effect during the 2026-27 academic year and is in collaboration with KeyTV, which Palmer co-founded in 2021.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated June 12 at 5:11 p.m._ Keke Palmer is taking on a new role in Westwood. The Emmy Award winner announced Thursday morning that she will begin a five-year Artist in Residency program with UCLA’s School of Theater, Film and Television, titled “From Blocking to Broadcast.” The program will go into effect during the 2026-27 academic year and is in collaboration with KeyTV, which Palmer co-founded in 2021.

item.title: This Year: 2025-26
item.description: Listen to Podcasts editor Megan Vahdat, News editor and incoming editor-in-chief Alexandra Crosnoe, Arts and Entertainment editor Eric Sican and Assistant Sports editor Grant Walters discuss the top headlines from the 2025-26 academic year.
item.source:markdown: Listen to Podcasts editor Megan Vahdat, News editor and incoming editor-in-chief Alexandra Crosnoe, Arts and Entertainment editor Eric Sican and Assistant Sports editor Grant Walters discuss the top headlines from the 2025-26 academic year.

Editorial Cartoon: “My degree can’t tell me who to be!”
Editorial Cartoon: “What we mean by college friends”
Gallery: Vacations brings its ‘Holy Grail Tour’ to the Troubadour with three-day run
item.description: Vacations performed three consecutive sold-out shows at the Troubadour from June 2 to June 4. The Australian indie band announced its “Holy Grail Tour” to promote the release of its fourth studio album, “Pursuit of Anything,” releasing Oct.
item.source:markdown: Vacations performed three consecutive sold-out shows at the Troubadour from June 2 to June 4. The Australian indie band announced its “Holy Grail Tour” to promote the release of its fourth studio album, “Pursuit of Anything,” releasing Oct.

item.title: The logistics of graduation at UCLA
item.description: What goes into planning the 73 graduation ceremonies for 15,000 UCLA graduates? The Daily Bruin brings you this look at the complex logistics of commencement. Videography: Nina Ali, Max Davis-Housefield, Abhi Kumar, Victoria Marchetti, Milo Misher and Celine Ngo Editor: Max Davis-Housefield Executive Producers: Max Davis-Housefield, Nina Ali and Nora Gallagher
item.source:markdown: What goes into planning the 73 graduation ceremonies for 15,000 UCLA graduates? The Daily Bruin brings you this look at the complex logistics of commencement. Videography: Nina Ali, Max Davis-Housefield, Abhi Kumar, Victoria Marchetti, Milo Misher and Celine Ngo Editor: Max Davis-Housefield Executive Producers: Max Davis-Housefield, Nina Ali and Nora Gallagher

item.title: Daily Bruin Graduation Issue – June 12, 2026
item.description: The Daily Bruin publishes print issues every Monday, Wednesday and Friday during the school year. You can view the online version of The Bruin here. ﻿
item.source:markdown: The Daily Bruin publishes print issues every Monday, Wednesday and Friday during the school year. You can view the online version of The Bruin here. ﻿

item.title: Students reflect on housing experiences amid floods, policy changes
item.description: Catherine Wunderly, a front desk supervisor for Hedrick Court, was asleep when she received six 1 a.m. emergency calls in early November. The next morning, the second-year neuroscience student said she had learned a flood had impacted Hedrick Hall – a dorm building in Hedrick Court – displacing 32 students from 11 dorm rooms.
item.source:markdown: Catherine Wunderly, a front desk supervisor for Hedrick Court, was asleep when she received six 1 a.m. emergency calls in early November. The next morning, the second-year neuroscience student said she had learned a flood had impacted Hedrick Hall – a dorm building in Hedrick Court – displacing 32 students from 11 dorm rooms.
